stories_covered: Golden age of software thesis: spend rising 2% to 4% of GDP
Revenue durability collapse for late-stage unicorns
AI startup valuations: Cursor $9.6B, Scale, Windsurf in froth
Founder fundraising crisis: 80% of VCs rejecting triple-triple-double-double
AI fraud: zero-to-multimillion in months via ChatGPT wrappers
CoreWeave IPO day one pop, first agentic infrastructure IPO
Hardware startups and whether 19-year-olds can compete with Nvidia
harry_takes: Revenue durability assumptions have collapsed—late-stage slowdowns will become normalized, not seen as founder failure
Venture has become a pure-growth-chase goldfish brain: 20x revenue multiples on anything with 99% MoM growth, margins and sustainability abandoned
Young founders almost never win in complex infrastructure/hardware; customer-intimacy categories reward inexperience, not chip design
Incumbent enterprises move slowly on AI adoption even when budgets exist—change management is the real bottleneck, not technology
IPO day one noise is meaningless; what matters is execution 6-12 months later
harry_boldest_quote: There's nothing weirder than being an employee the day after the IPO, right?
predictions: Lemkin: Figma, Canva, Stripe, Databricks will IPO in next 18 months (told by at least one CEO eyeing 18-month exit)
Lemkin: M&A wave incoming if Trump stays pro-M&A and avoids Europe regulatory blocks
Lemkin: 400 PE firms have appetite to nibble, but he's seeing zero tire-kicking on his portfolio—very worried about PE-via-consolidation liquidity myth
Feldman: Hardware requires gray hair and accumulated wisdom; inexperienced young founders historically fail vs. social networks where they thrive
disagreements: Harry skeptical Figma/Canva actually IPO in 18 months (they can run private forever with secondary markets). Lemkin insists at least one CEO told him 18-month IPO plan. Mild tension on whether PE is real or theater for founder liquidity.
harry_role: Harry moderates as the host, driving question-based pacing. He defers to Lemkin's pattern-recognition on VC trends, asks clarifying questions of guests (Barvin, Feldman), and occasionally surfaces his own observations (durability collapse, post-IPO weirdness, skepticism on Figma/Canva near-term IPOs). Not a debate equal—more ringmaster.
notable: CoreWeave IPO occurred the day before this recording (day-one pop noted); Barvin live discusses the freshly-announced ServiceNow deal with unusual candor about partnership overlap and regulatory risk; frank admission of AI fraud in startup ecosystem (ChatGPT wrappers sold as AI, humans running prompts).
